summary: Uncle Bob responds to confusion and criticism from his previous post about classes in functional programming by providing a concrete Clojure code example. Using a 'Dilithium Cloud' class from his spacewar game, he demonstrates that classes can exist in functional programming without keywords, polymorphism, or inheritance. He defines a class as 'an intentionally named abstraction that consists of a set of narrowly cohesive functions that operate over an internally defined data structure.' The post playfully mocks static typing purists while showing how dynamic type checking, constructors, and methods can all exist in a functional paradigm.
key_points: A class doesn't require the class keyword, polymorphic dispatch, or inheritance - it's simply a description of an object
Types can be defined dynamically in Clojure using specs, with validation functions for testing
Functional methods differ from traditional OO methods by operating on collections, avoiding polymorphism, and returning new immutable objects
Cohesive functions operating over an internal data structure within a named abstraction constitute a class regardless of paradigm
The OO vs FP distinction is less fundamental than purists on either side suggest
money_quotes: A class is an intentionally named abstraction that consists of a set of narrowly cohesive functions that operate over an internally defined data structure.
We do not need the class keyword. Nor do we need polymorphic dispatch. Nor do we need inheritance. A class is just a description, whether in full or in part, of an object.

To all the adherents of the Statically Typed Functional Programming religion: I know that you believe that Static Typing is an essential aspect of Functional Programming and that no mere dynamically typed language could ever begin to approach the heights and glory of The One True and Holy TYPED Functional Apotheotic Paradigm.
So are these really methods of the cloud class? Sure! Why not? They are a set of narrowly cohesive functions that manipulate an internal data structure within an intentionally named abstraction.
key_insight: The concept of a class transcends language paradigms - it's fundamentally about organizing cohesive functions around a data structure, whether you call it OO or functional programming.
